questions.question: What is the primary function of shutter speed?
options: Control the amount of light entering the lens
Determine the duration the sensor is exposed to light
Change the focal length of the lens

questions.question: Which shutter speed is most likely to freeze fast motion?
What effect does a slow shutter speed have on a moving subject?
Which shutter speed takes the longest duration to collect light?
To capture a silky waterfall with smooth, blurred water, which shutter speed range works best?
questions.reviewHint: Silky water needs a slow shutter — the guide suggests roughly 1/4–2 seconds (use a tripod).

questions.question: In which camera mode does the photographer set the shutter speed while the camera selects the appropriate aperture?
Which shutter speed is best for a sharp photo of someone running?
questions.reviewHint: Fast motion like running needs a fast shutter — around 1/1000s (the page lists sport at 1/1000–1/2000s).

questions.question: What is the effect of using a shutter speed slower than 1/60 without a tripod?
options: Camera shake leading to a blurry image

questions.question: Which shutter speed takes the shortest duration to collect light?
What is the term for the technique of using a slow shutter speed to capture light trails from moving vehicles at night?
What rule does the guide recommend for the slowest handheld shutter speed to avoid camera shake?
options: At least 1 / (2 × the lens focal length)
The same number as your ISO
questions.reviewHint: The guide's handheld rule is to start at 1/(2× focal length) — e.g. ~1/100s for a 50mm lens.

questions.question: What happens to the exposure when you decrease the shutter speed from 1/500 to 1/250, with no changes to aperture and ISO?
